"""Domain entities — pure data shapes. No I/O, no third-party types."""

from __future__ import annotations

from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from datetime import datetime
from enum import Enum
from pathlib import Path


class Platform(str, Enum):
    ANDROID = "android"
    IOS = "ios"


class DeviceState(str, Enum):
    DEVICE = "device"
    OFFLINE = "offline"
    UNAUTHORIZED = "unauthorized"
    UNKNOWN = "unknown"


class BuildMode(str, Enum):
    DEBUG = "debug"
    PROFILE = "profile"
    RELEASE = "release"


class LogLevel(str, Enum):
    VERBOSE = "V"
    DEBUG = "D"
    INFO = "I"
    WARN = "W"
    ERROR = "E"
    FATAL = "F"


class TestStatus(str, Enum):
    PASSED = "passed"
    FAILED = "failed"
    ERRORED = "errored"
    SKIPPED = "skipped"


class ArtifactKind(str, Enum):
    SCREENSHOT = "screenshot"
    RECORDING = "recording"
    LOG = "log"
    UI_DUMP = "ui_dump"
    REPORT = "report"


@dataclass(frozen=True, slots=True)
class Device:
    serial: str
    state: DeviceState
    model: str | None = None
    os_version: str | None = None
    platform: Platform | None = None


@dataclass(frozen=True, slots=True)
class AppBundle:
    """A built application artifact — APK on Android, IPA/.app on iOS."""

    path: Path
    mode: BuildMode
    platform: Platform = Platform.ANDROID
    flavor: str | None = None


@dataclass(frozen=True, slots=True)
class Bounds:
    x: int
    y: int
    width: int
    height: int

    @property
    def center(self) -> tuple[int, int]:
        return (self.x + self.width // 2, self.y + self.height // 2)


@dataclass(frozen=True, slots=True)
class UiElement:
    text: str | None
    resource_id: str | None
    class_name: str | None
    content_description: str | None
    bounds: Bounds
    enabled: bool
    clickable: bool


@dataclass(frozen=True, slots=True)
class LogEntry:
    timestamp: str
    level: LogLevel
    tag: str
    pid: int | None
    message: str


@dataclass(frozen=True, slots=True)
class TestCase:
    name: str
    status: TestStatus
    duration_ms: int
    error_message: str | None = None
    stack_trace: str | None = None


@dataclass(frozen=True, slots=True)
class TestRun:
    total: int
    passed: int
    failed: int
    errored: int
    skipped: int
    duration_ms: int
    cases: list[TestCase] = field(default_factory=list)

    @property
    def is_success(self) -> bool:
        return self.failed == 0 and self.errored == 0


@dataclass(frozen=True, slots=True)
class Artifact:
    path: Path
    kind: ArtifactKind
    label: str | None = None


@dataclass(frozen=True, slots=True)
class Session:
    id: str
    root: Path
    started_at: datetime
    label: str | None = None

# Code Quality Rules

- Always analyze existing code before modifying it — understand patterns before changing them.
- Use type-safe patterns: prefer Either/Result types over throwing exceptions.
- Write tests alongside implementation, not as an afterthought.
- Follow the existing naming conventions in the codebase.
- Keep functions under 30 lines; extract complex logic into well-named helpers.
- Never commit secrets, API keys, or credentials in code.
- Use const/final/readonly where possible to signal intent.

    UNAUTHORIZED = "unauthorized"
    UNKNOWN = "unknown"


class BuildMode(str, Enum):
    DEBUG = "debug"
    PROFILE = "profile"
    RELEASE = "release"


class LogLevel(str, Enum):
    VERBOSE = "V"
    DEBUG = "D"
    INFO = "I"
    WARN = "W"
    ERROR = "E"
    FATAL = "F"


class TestStatus(str, Enum):
    PASSED = "passed"
    FAILED = "failed"
    ERRORED = "errored"
    SKIPPED = "skipped"


class ArtifactKind(str, Enum):
    SCREENSHOT = "screenshot"
    RECORDING = "recording"
    LOG = "log"
    UI_DUMP = "ui_dump"
    REPORT = "report"


@dataclass(frozen=True, slots=True)
class Device:
    serial: str
    state: DeviceState
    model: str | None = None
    android_version: str | None = None


@dataclass(frozen=True, slots=True)
class ApkArtifact:
    path: Path
    mode: BuildMode
    flavor: str | None = None


@dataclass(frozen=True, slots=True)
class Bounds:
    x: int
    y: int
    width: int
    height: int

    @property
    def center(self) -> tuple[int, int]:
        return (self.x + self.width // 2, self.y + self.height // 2)


@dataclass(frozen=True, slots=True)
class UiElement:
    text: str | None
    resource_id: str | None
    class_name: str | None
    content_description: str | None
    bounds: Bounds
    enabled: bool
    clickable: bool


@dataclass(frozen=True, slots=True)
class LogEntry:
    timestamp: str
    level: LogLevel
    tag: str
    pid: int | None
    message: str


@dataclass(frozen=True, slots=True)
class TestCase:
    name: str
    status: TestStatus
    duration_ms: int
    error_message: str | None = None
    stack_trace: str | None = None


@dataclass(frozen=True, slots=True)
class TestRun:
    total: int
    passed: int
    failed: int
    errored: int
    skipped: int
    duration_ms: int
    cases: list[TestCase] = field(default_factory=list)

    @property
    def is_success(self) -> bool:
        return self.failed == 0 and self.errored == 0


@dataclass(frozen=True, slots=True)
class Artifact:
    path: Path
    kind: ArtifactKind
    label: str | None = None


@dataclass(frozen=True, slots=True)
class Session:
    id: str
    root: Path
    started_at: datetime
    label: str | None = None

"""Repository protocols — the boundary use cases depend on. Implemented in `data/`."""

from __future__ import annotations

from pathlib import Path
from typing import Protocol, runtime_checkable

from .entities import (
    ApkArtifact,
    Artifact,
    BuildMode,
    Device,
    LogEntry,
    LogLevel,
    Session,
    TestRun,
    UiElement,
)
from .result import Result


@runtime_checkable
class DeviceRepository(Protocol):
    async def list_devices(self) -> Result[list[Device]]: ...
    async def get_device(self, serial: str) -> Result[Device]: ...


@runtime_checkable
class LifecycleRepository(Protocol):
    async def install(self, serial: str, apk_path: Path, replace: bool = True) -> Result[None]: ...
    async def uninstall(self, serial: str, package_id: str) -> Result[None]: ...
    async def launch(
        self, serial: str, package_id: str, activity: str | None = None
    ) -> Result[None]: ...
    async def stop(self, serial: str, package_id: str) -> Result[None]: ...
    async def clear_data(self, serial: str, package_id: str) -> Result[None]: ...
    async def grant_permission(
        self, serial: str, package_id: str, permission: str
    ) -> Result[None]: ...


@runtime_checkable
class BuildRepository(Protocol):
    async def build_apk(
        self, project_path: Path, mode: BuildMode, flavor: str | None = None
    ) -> Result[ApkArtifact]: ...


@runtime_checkable
class UiRepository(Protocol):
    async def tap(self, serial: str, x: int, y: int) -> Result[None]: ...
    async def tap_text(self, serial: str, text: str, exact: bool = False) -> Result[None]: ...
    async def swipe(
        self, serial: str, x1: int, y1: int, x2: int, y2: int, duration_ms: int = 300
    ) -> Result[None]: ...
    async def type_text(self, serial: str, text: str) -> Result[None]: ...
    async def press_key(self, serial: str, keycode: str) -> Result[None]: ...
    async def find(
        self,
        serial: str,
        text: str | None = None,
        resource_id: str | None = None,
        class_name: str | None = None,
        timeout_s: float = 5.0,
    ) -> Result[UiElement | None]: ...
    async def wait_for(
        self,
        serial: str,
        text: str | None = None,
        resource_id: str | None = None,
        timeout_s: float = 10.0,
    ) -> Result[UiElement]: ...
    async def dump_ui(self, serial: str) -> Result[str]: ...


@runtime_checkable
class ObservationRepository(Protocol):
    async def screenshot(self, serial: str, output_path: Path) -> Result[Path]: ...
    async def start_recording(self, serial: str, output_path: Path) -> Result[None]: ...
    async def stop_recording(self, serial: str) -> Result[Path]: ...
    async def read_logs(
        self,
        serial: str,
        since_s: int = 30,
        tag: str | None = None,
        min_level: LogLevel = LogLevel.WARN,
        max_lines: int = 500,
    ) -> Result[list[LogEntry]]: ...
    async def tail_logs_until(
        self,
        serial: str,
        until_pattern: str,
        tag: str | None = None,
        timeout_s: float = 30.0,
    ) -> Result[list[LogEntry]]: ...


@runtime_checkable
class TestRepository(Protocol):
    async def run_unit_tests(self, project_path: Path) -> Result[TestRun]: ...
    async def run_integration_tests(
        self,
        project_path: Path,
        device_serial: str,
        test_path: str = "integration_test/",
    ) -> Result[TestRun]: ...


@runtime_checkable
class ArtifactRepository(Protocol):
    async def new_session(self, label: str | None = None) -> Result[Session]: ...
    async def current_session(self) -> Result[Session]: ...
    async def allocate_path(
        self, kind: str, suffix: str, label: str | None = None
    ) -> Result[Path]: ...
    async def register(self, artifact: Artifact) -> Result[None]: ...


@runtime_checkable
class SessionStateRepository(Protocol):
    """Holds the currently selected device serial across MCP tool calls."""

    async def set_selected_serial(self, serial: str | None) -> Result[None]: ...
    async def get_selected_serial(self) -> Result[str | None]: ...

toolUseResult.originalFile:
"""Async wrapper around the `flutter` CLI."""

from __future__ import annotations

from pathlib import Path

from .process_runner import ProcessResult, ProcessRunner


class FlutterCli:
    def __init__(self, runner: ProcessRunner, flutter_path: str = "flutter") -> None:
        self._runner = runner
        self._flutter = flutter_path

    async def build_apk(
        self,
        project_path: Path,
        mode: str = "debug",
        flavor: str | None = None,
        timeout_s: float = 600.0,
    ) -> ProcessResult:
        argv = [self._flutter, "build", "apk", f"--{mode}"]
        if flavor:
            argv += ["--flavor", flavor]
        return await self._runner.run(argv, cwd=project_path, timeout_s=timeout_s)

    async def test_unit(
        self, project_path: Path, timeout_s: float = 600.0
    ) -> ProcessResult:
        return await self._runner.run(
            [self._flutter, "test", "--reporter=json"],
            cwd=project_path,
            timeout_s=timeout_s,
        )

    async def test_integration(
        self,
        project_path: Path,
        device_serial: str,
        test_path: str = "integration_test/",
        timeout_s: float = 1800.0,
    ) -> ProcessResult:
        return await self._runner.run(
            [
                self._flutter,
                "test",
                test_path,
                "-d",
                device_serial,
                "--reporter=json",
            ],
            cwd=project_path,
            timeout_s=timeout_s,
        )

"""Lazy facebook-wda Client factory keyed by udid. Caches sessions.

Connection is over usbmux (no iproxy needed) — just `wda.USBClient(udid)`.
The WebDriverAgent app must already be built and installed on the device.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import asyncio
from typing import Any, Protocol


class WdaFactory(Protocol):
    async def get(self, udid: str) -> Any: ...


class CachingWdaFactory:
    def __init__(self) -> None:
        self._clients: dict[str, Any] = {}
        self._lock = asyncio.Lock()

    async def get(self, udid: str) -> Any:
        async with self._lock:
            if udid in self._clients:
                return self._clients[udid]
            import wda  # local import — heavy + optional at test time

            client = await asyncio.to_thread(wda.USBClient, udid)
            session = await asyncio.to_thread(client.session)
            self._clients[udid] = session
            return session

The WebDriverAgent app must already be built and installed on the device.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import asyncio
from typing import Any, Protocol


class WdaFactory(Protocol):
    async def get(self, udid: str) -> Any: ...


class CachingWdaFactory:
    def __init__(self) -> None:
        self._clients: dict[str, Any] = {}
        self._lock = asyncio.Lock()

    async def get(self, udid: str) -> Any:
        async with self._lock:
            if udid in self._clients:
                return self._clients[udid]
            import wda  # local import — heavy + optional at test time

            client = await asyncio.to_thread(wda.USBClient, udid)
            session = await asyncio.to_thread(client.session)
            self._clients[udid] = session
            return session

"""Composition root. Wires Android + iOS concrete repositories behind composites."""

from __future__ import annotations

from pathlib import Path

from .data.repositories.adb_device_repository import AdbDeviceRepository
from .data.repositories.adb_lifecycle_repository import AdbLifecycleRepository
from .data.repositories.adb_observation_repository import AdbObservationRepository
from .data.repositories.composite.composite_repositories import (
    CompositeBuildRepository,
    CompositeDeviceRepository,
    CompositeLifecycleRepository,
    CompositeObservationRepository,
    CompositeTestRepository,
    CompositeUiRepository,
)
from .data.repositories.composite.platform_resolver import CachingPlatformResolver
from .data.repositories.filesystem_artifact_repository import FilesystemArtifactRepository
from .data.repositories.flutter_build_repository import FlutterBuildRepository
from .data.repositories.flutter_test_repository import FlutterTestRepository
from .data.repositories.in_memory_session_state_repository import (
    InMemorySessionStateRepository,
)
from .data.repositories.ios_device_repository import IosDeviceRepository
from .data.repositories.ios_lifecycle_repository import IosLifecycleRepository
from .data.repositories.ios_observation_repository import IosObservationRepository
from .data.repositories.uiautomator2_ui_repository import UiAutomator2UiRepository
from .data.repositories.wda_ui_repository import WdaUiRepository
from .domain.usecases.artifacts import GetArtifactsDir, NewSession
from .domain.usecases.build_install import BuildApp, InstallApp, UninstallApp
from .domain.usecases.devices import GetSelectedDevice, ListDevices, SelectDevice
from .domain.usecases.lifecycle import (
    ClearAppData,
    GrantPermission,
    LaunchApp,
    StopApp,
)
from .domain.usecases.observation import (
    ReadLogs,
    StartRecording,
    StopRecording,
    TailLogs,
    TakeScreenshot,
)
from .domain.usecases.testing import RunIntegrationTests, RunUnitTests
from .domain.usecases.ui_input import PressKey, Swipe, Tap, TapText, TypeText
from .domain.usecases.ui_query import AssertVisible, DumpUi, FindElement, WaitForElement
from .infrastructure.adb_client import AdbClient
from .infrastructure.flutter_cli import FlutterCli
from .infrastructure.process_runner import AsyncProcessRunner
from .infrastructure.pymobiledevice3_cli import PyMobileDevice3Cli
from .infrastructure.uiautomator2_factory import CachingUiAutomator2Factory
from .infrastructure.wda_factory import CachingWdaFactory
from .presentation.tool_registry import UseCases, build_registry


def build_use_cases(artifacts_root: Path | None = None) -> UseCases:
    runner = AsyncProcessRunner()

    # Infrastructure clients
    adb = AdbClient(runner)
    flutter = FlutterCli(runner)
    u2_factory = CachingUiAutomator2Factory()
    pmd3 = PyMobileDevice3Cli(runner)
    wda_factory = CachingWdaFactory()

    # Per-platform repositories
    android_devices = AdbDeviceRepository(adb)
    android_lifecycle = AdbLifecycleRepository(adb)
    android_ui = UiAutomator2UiRepository(u2_factory)
    android_observation = AdbObservationRepository(adb)

    ios_devices = IosDeviceRepository(pmd3)
    ios_lifecycle = IosLifecycleRepository(pmd3)
    ios_ui = WdaUiRepository(wda_factory)
    ios_observation = IosObservationRepository(pmd3)

    # Build & test are platform-agnostic at the call site; the Flutter repos
    # internally branch on Platform. We still pass them through composites so
    # the routing logic is uniform and a future iOS-specific override is trivial.
    flutter_build = FlutterBuildRepository(flutter)
    flutter_tests = FlutterTestRepository(flutter)

    # Routing
    resolver = CachingPlatformResolver()
    devices_repo = CompositeDeviceRepository(android_devices, ios_devices, resolver)
    lifecycle_repo = CompositeLifecycleRepository(android_lifecycle, ios_lifecycle, resolver)
    ui_repo = CompositeUiRepository(android_ui, ios_ui, resolver)
    observation_repo = CompositeObservationRepository(
        android_observation, ios_observation, resolver
    )
    build_repo = CompositeBuildRepository(flutter_build, flutter_build)
    test_repo = CompositeTestRepository(flutter_tests, flutter_tests, resolver)

    # Cross-cutting
    artifacts_repo = FilesystemArtifactRepository(
        artifacts_root or Path.home() / ".mcp_phone_controll" / "sessions"
    )
    state_repo = InMemorySessionStateRepository()

    return UseCases(
        list_devices=ListDevices(devices_repo),
        select_device=SelectDevice(devices_repo, state_repo),
        get_selected_device=GetSelectedDevice(devices_repo, state_repo),
        build_app=BuildApp(build_repo),
        install_app=InstallApp(build_repo, lifecycle_repo, devices_repo, state_repo),
        uninstall_app=UninstallApp(lifecycle_repo, state_repo),
        launch_app=LaunchApp(lifecycle_repo, state_repo),
        stop_app=StopApp(lifecycle_repo, state_repo),
        clear_app_data=ClearAppData(lifecycle_repo, state_repo),
        grant_permission=GrantPermission(lifecycle_repo, state_repo),
        tap=Tap(ui_repo, state_repo),
        tap_text=TapText(ui_repo, state_repo),
        swipe=Swipe(ui_repo, state_repo),
        type_text=TypeText(ui_repo, state_repo),
        press_key=PressKey(ui_repo, state_repo),
        find_element=FindElement(ui_repo, state_repo),
        wait_for_element=WaitForElement(ui_repo, state_repo),
        dump_ui=DumpUi(ui_repo, state_repo),
        assert_visible=AssertVisible(ui_repo, state_repo),
        take_screenshot=TakeScreenshot(observation_repo, artifacts_repo, state_repo),
        start_recording=StartRecording(observation_repo, artifacts_repo, state_repo),
        stop_recording=StopRecording(observation_repo, artifacts_repo, state_repo),
        read_logs=ReadLogs(observation_repo, state_repo),
        tail_logs=TailLogs(observation_repo, state_repo),
        run_unit_tests=RunUnitTests(test_repo),
        run_integration_tests=RunIntegrationTests(test_repo, state_repo),
        new_session=NewSession(artifacts_repo),
        get_artifacts_dir=GetArtifactsDir(artifacts_repo),
    )


def build_descriptors(artifacts_root: Path | None = None):
    return build_registry(build_use_cases(artifacts_root))

        new_session=NewSession(artifacts_repo),
        get_artifacts_dir=GetArtifactsDir(artifacts_repo),
    )


def build_descriptors(artifacts_root: Path | None = None):

"""Use-case tests against in-memory fakes."""

from __future__ import annotations

from pathlib import Path

import pytest

from mcp_phone_controll.domain.entities import BuildMode, UiElement, Bounds
from mcp_phone_controll.domain.failures import (
    DeviceNotFoundFailure,
    NoDeviceSelectedFailure,
    UiElementNotFoundFailure,
)
from mcp_phone_controll.domain.result import Err, Ok
from mcp_phone_controll.domain.usecases.base import NoParams
from mcp_phone_controll.domain.usecases.build_install import (
    InstallApp,
    InstallAppParams,
)
from mcp_phone_controll.domain.usecases.devices import (
    GetSelectedDevice,
    ListDevices,
    SelectDevice,
    SelectDeviceParams,
)
from mcp_phone_controll.domain.usecases.lifecycle import (
    LaunchApp,
    LaunchAppParams,
)
from mcp_phone_controll.domain.usecases.ui_query import (
    AssertVisible,
    AssertVisibleParams,
)
from tests.fakes.fake_repositories import (
    FakeArtifactRepository,
    FakeBuildRepository,
    FakeDeviceRepository,
    FakeLifecycleRepository,
    FakeSessionStateRepository,
    FakeUiRepository,
)


@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_list_devices_returns_devices():
    uc = ListDevices(FakeDeviceRepository())
    result = await uc(NoParams())
    assert isinstance(result, Ok)
    assert result.value[0].serial == "EMU01"


@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_select_device_persists_selection():
    devices = FakeDeviceRepository()
    state = FakeSessionStateRepository()
    uc = SelectDevice(devices, state)
    result = await uc(SelectDeviceParams(serial="EMU01"))
    assert isinstance(result, Ok)
    assert state.serial == "EMU01"


@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_select_device_unknown_returns_err():
    uc = SelectDevice(FakeDeviceRepository(), FakeSessionStateRepository())
    result = await uc(SelectDeviceParams(serial="NOPE"))
    assert isinstance(result, Err)
    assert isinstance(result.failure, DeviceNotFoundFailure)


@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_get_selected_device_when_unselected_returns_none():
    uc = GetSelectedDevice(FakeDeviceRepository(), FakeSessionStateRepository())
    result = await uc(NoParams())
    assert isinstance(result, Ok)
    assert result.value is None


@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_install_uses_selected_serial(tmp_path: Path):
    apk = tmp_path / "fake.apk"
    apk.write_bytes(b"X")
    builds = FakeBuildRepository(apk_path=apk)
    lifecycle = FakeLifecycleRepository()
    state = FakeSessionStateRepository(serial="EMU01")
    uc = InstallApp(builds, lifecycle, state)

    result = await uc(InstallAppParams(apk_path=apk))
    assert isinstance(result, Ok)
    assert any(call[0] == "install" and call[1] == "EMU01" for call in lifecycle.calls)


@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_install_without_selected_device_errors(tmp_path: Path):
    builds = FakeBuildRepository()
    lifecycle = FakeLifecycleRepository()
    state = FakeSessionStateRepository()
    uc = InstallApp(builds, lifecycle, state)

    result = await uc(InstallAppParams(apk_path=tmp_path / "x.apk"))
    assert isinstance(result, Err)
    assert isinstance(result.failure, NoDeviceSelectedFailure)


@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_install_builds_when_only_project_path_given():
    builds = FakeBuildRepository(apk_path=Path("/tmp/built.apk"))
    lifecycle = FakeLifecycleRepository()
    state = FakeSessionStateRepository(serial="EMU01")
    uc = InstallApp(builds, lifecycle, state)

    result = await uc(
        InstallAppParams(project_path=Path("/work/myapp"), mode=BuildMode.DEBUG)
    )
    assert isinstance(result, Ok)
    assert result.value.path == Path("/tmp/built.apk")


@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_launch_passes_activity():
    lifecycle = FakeLifecycleRepository()
    state = FakeSessionStateRepository(serial="EMU01")
    uc = LaunchApp(lifecycle, state)

    result = await uc(LaunchAppParams(package_id="com.x", activity=".Main"))
    assert isinstance(result, Ok)
    assert ("launch", "EMU01", "com.x", ".Main") in lifecycle.calls


@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_assert_visible_succeeds_when_found():
    elem = UiElement(
        text="Sign in",
        resource_id=None,
        class_name="android.widget.Button",
        content_description=None,
        bounds=Bounds(0, 0, 100, 50),
        enabled=True,
        clickable=True,
    )
    ui = FakeUiRepository(found=elem)
    state = FakeSessionStateRepository(serial="EMU01")
    uc = AssertVisible(ui, state)
    result = await uc(AssertVisibleParams(text="Sign in"))
    assert isinstance(result, Ok)
    assert result.value is elem


@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_assert_visible_errors_when_missing():
    ui = FakeUiRepository(found=None)
    state = FakeSessionStateRepository(serial="EMU01")
    uc = AssertVisible(ui, state)
    result = await uc(AssertVisibleParams(text="Nope"))
    assert isinstance(result, Err)
    assert isinstance(result.failure, UiElementNotFoundFailure)


@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_artifact_session_returned_by_fake(tmp_path: Path):
    artifacts = FakeArtifactRepository(root=tmp_path)
    res = await artifacts.new_session("smoke")
    assert isinstance(res, Ok)
    assert res.value.label == "smoke"
    assert res.value.root.exists()

"""End-to-end dispatcher tests using fake repositories all the way through."""

from __future__ import annotations

from pathlib import Path

import pytest

from mcp_phone_controll.domain.usecases.artifacts import GetArtifactsDir, NewSession
from mcp_phone_controll.domain.usecases.build_install import (
    BuildApp,
    InstallApp,
    UninstallApp,
)
from mcp_phone_controll.domain.usecases.devices import (
    GetSelectedDevice,
    ListDevices,
    SelectDevice,
)
from mcp_phone_controll.domain.usecases.lifecycle import (
    ClearAppData,
    GrantPermission,
    LaunchApp,
    StopApp,
)
from mcp_phone_controll.domain.usecases.observation import (
    ReadLogs,
    StartRecording,
    StopRecording,
    TailLogs,
    TakeScreenshot,
)
from mcp_phone_controll.domain.usecases.testing import (
    RunIntegrationTests,
    RunUnitTests,
)
from mcp_phone_controll.domain.usecases.ui_input import (
    PressKey,
    Swipe,
    Tap,
    TapText,
    TypeText,
)
from mcp_phone_controll.domain.usecases.ui_query import (
    AssertVisible,
    DumpUi,
    FindElement,
    WaitForElement,
)
from mcp_phone_controll.presentation.tool_registry import (
    ToolDispatcher,
    UseCases,
    build_registry,
)
from tests.fakes.fake_repositories import (
    FakeArtifactRepository,
    FakeBuildRepository,
    FakeDeviceRepository,
    FakeLifecycleRepository,
    FakeObservationRepository,
    FakeSessionStateRepository,
    FakeTestRepository,
    FakeUiRepository,
)


def _build_fake_dispatcher(tmp_path: Path) -> ToolDispatcher:
    devices = FakeDeviceRepository()
    lifecycle = FakeLifecycleRepository()
    builds = FakeBuildRepository(apk_path=tmp_path / "fake.apk")
    (tmp_path / "fake.apk").write_bytes(b"X")
    ui = FakeUiRepository()
    observation = FakeObservationRepository()
    tests = FakeTestRepository()
    artifacts = FakeArtifactRepository(root=tmp_path / "sessions")
    state = FakeSessionStateRepository()

    use_cases = UseCases(
        list_devices=ListDevices(devices),
        select_device=SelectDevice(devices, state),
        get_selected_device=GetSelectedDevice(devices, state),
        build_app=BuildApp(builds),
        install_app=InstallApp(builds, lifecycle, state),
        uninstall_app=UninstallApp(lifecycle, state),
        launch_app=LaunchApp(lifecycle, state),
        stop_app=StopApp(lifecycle, state),
        clear_app_data=ClearAppData(lifecycle, state),
        grant_permission=GrantPermission(lifecycle, state),
        tap=Tap(ui, state),
        tap_text=TapText(ui, state),
        swipe=Swipe(ui, state),
        type_text=TypeText(ui, state),
        press_key=PressKey(ui, state),
        find_element=FindElement(ui, state),
        wait_for_element=WaitForElement(ui, state),
        dump_ui=DumpUi(ui, state),
        assert_visible=AssertVisible(ui, state),
        take_screenshot=TakeScreenshot(observation, artifacts, state),
        start_recording=StartRecording(observation, artifacts, state),
        stop_recording=StopRecording(observation, artifacts, state),
        read_logs=ReadLogs(observation, state),
        tail_logs=TailLogs(observation, state),
        run_unit_tests=RunUnitTests(tests),
        run_integration_tests=RunIntegrationTests(tests, state),
        new_session=NewSession(artifacts),
        get_artifacts_dir=GetArtifactsDir(artifacts),
    )
    return ToolDispatcher(build_registry(use_cases))


@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_full_smoke_loop(tmp_path: Path):
    dispatcher = _build_fake_dispatcher(tmp_path)

    # 1. list_devices
    res = await dispatcher.dispatch("list_devices", {})
    assert res["ok"] is True
    assert res["data"][0]["serial"] == "EMU01"

    # 2. select_device
    res = await dispatcher.dispatch("select_device", {"serial": "EMU01"})
    assert res["ok"] is True

    # 3. install via project_path (uses fake build)
    res = await dispatcher.dispatch(
        "install_app", {"project_path": str(tmp_path), "mode": "debug"}
    )
    assert res["ok"] is True
    assert res["data"]["mode"] == "debug"

    # 4. launch
    res = await dispatcher.dispatch("launch_app", {"package_id": "com.example"})
    assert res["ok"] is True

    # 5. tap_text
    res = await dispatcher.dispatch("tap_text", {"text": "Sign in"})
    assert res["ok"] is True

    # 6. screenshot
    res = await dispatcher.dispatch("take_screenshot", {"label": "after-sign-in"})
    assert res["ok"] is True
    assert Path(res["data"]).exists()

    # 7. read_logs
    res = await dispatcher.dispatch("read_logs", {"since_s": 5})
    assert res["ok"] is True
    assert isinstance(res["data"], list)

    # 8. run_integration_tests
    res = await dispatcher.dispatch(
        "run_integration_tests", {"project_path": str(tmp_path)}
    )
    assert res["ok"] is True
    assert res["data"]["passed"] == 1


@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_unknown_tool_returns_error(tmp_path: Path):
    dispatcher = _build_fake_dispatcher(tmp_path)
    res = await dispatcher.dispatch("nonexistent", {})
    assert res["ok"] is False
    assert res["error"]["code"] == "UnknownTool"

    dispatcher = _build_fake_dispatcher(tmp_path)

input.content:
import json

from mcp_phone_controll.data.parsers.pymobiledevice3_parser import parse_usbmux_list
from mcp_phone_controll.domain.entities import DeviceState, Platform


def test_parses_single_device_json_array():
    payload = json.dumps(
        [
            {
                "ConnectionType": "USB",
                "Identifier": "00008101-001A4D2A14C8001E",
                "DeviceName": "Michal's iPhone",
                "ProductType": "iPhone14,2",
                "ProductVersion": "17.4.1",
                "BuildVersion": "21E236",
            }
        ]
    )
    devices = parse_usbmux_list(payload)
    assert len(devices) == 1
    d = devices[0]
    assert d.serial == "00008101-001A4D2A14C8001E"
    assert d.platform is Platform.IOS
    assert d.state is DeviceState.DEVICE
    assert d.os_version == "17.4.1"
    assert d.model == "iPhone14,2"


def test_parses_multiple_devices():
    payload = json.dumps(
        [
            {"Identifier": "udid-1", "ProductType": "iPhone15,3", "ProductVersion": "17.0"},
            {"Identifier": "udid-2", "ProductType": "iPad13,8", "ProductVersion": "16.5"},
        ]
    )
    devices = parse_usbmux_list(payload)
    assert [d.serial for d in devices] == ["udid-1", "udid-2"]
    assert all(d.platform is Platform.IOS for d in devices)


def test_returns_empty_on_empty_array():
    assert parse_usbmux_list("[]") == []
    assert parse_usbmux_list("") == []


def test_falls_back_when_logs_precede_json():
    raw = (
        "INFO:root:Connecting...\n"
        "DEBUG: ignored chatter\n"
        + json.dumps([{"Identifier": "udid-x", "ProductType": "iPhone14,1", "ProductVersion": "17.1"}])
    )
    devices = parse_usbmux_list(raw)
    assert len(devices) == 1
    assert devices[0].serial == "udid-x"


def test_uses_serialnumber_when_identifier_missing():
    payload = json.dumps([{"SerialNumber": "ABC123", "ProductType": "iPad14,1"}])
    devices = parse_usbmux_list(payload)
    assert devices[0].serial == "ABC123"


def test_skips_entries_without_serial():
    payload = json.dumps([{"ProductType": "iPhone14,1"}, {"Identifier": "ok"}])
    devices = parse_usbmux_list(payload)
    assert [d.serial for d in devices] == ["ok"]

"""Composite repositories must route by serial → platform.

These tests prove that:
- list_devices unions both backends and updates the resolver
- per-call repos route to the correct underlying impl
- unknown serials fail with DeviceNotFoundFailure
- iOS toolchain absence does not break Android paths
"""

from __future__ import annotations

from pathlib import Path

import pytest

from mcp_phone_controll.data.repositories.composite.composite_repositories import (
    CompositeBuildRepository,
    CompositeDeviceRepository,
    CompositeLifecycleRepository,
    CompositeObservationRepository,
    CompositeTestRepository,
    CompositeUiRepository,
)

"""Domain entity → JSON-serialisable dict. The MCP layer's only contact with entities."""

from __future__ import annotations

from dataclasses import is_dataclass
from enum import Enum
from pathlib import Path
from typing import Any

from ..domain.entities import (
    ApkArtifact,
    Artifact,
    Bounds,
    Device,
    LogEntry,
    Session,
    TestCase,
    TestRun,
    UiElement,
)


def to_jsonable(value: Any) -> Any:
    if value is None or isinstance(value, (str, int, float, bool)):
        return value
    if isinstance(value, Path):
        return str(value)
    if isinstance(value, Enum):
        return value.value
    if isinstance(value, list):
        return [to_jsonable(v) for v in value]
    if isinstance(value, tuple):
        return [to_jsonable(v) for v in value]
    if isinstance(value, dict):
        return {str(k): to_jsonable(v) for k, v in value.items()}
    if is_dataclass(value):
        return {f: to_jsonable(getattr(value, f)) for f in _dataclass_fields(value)}
    if hasattr(value, "isoformat"):
        return value.isoformat()
    return str(value)


def _dataclass_fields(value: Any) -> list[str]:
    if hasattr(value, "__dataclass_fields__"):
        return list(value.__dataclass_fields__.keys())
    return []


# Convenience aliases — every public entity goes through to_jsonable.
serialize_device = to_jsonable
serialize_apk = to_jsonable
serialize_bounds = to_jsonable
serialize_ui_element = to_jsonable
serialize_log_entry = to_jsonable
serialize_test_run = to_jsonable
serialize_test_case = to_jsonable
serialize_artifact = to_jsonable
serialize_session = to_jsonable

__all__ = [
    "to_jsonable",
    "serialize_device",
    "serialize_apk",
    "serialize_bounds",
    "serialize_ui_element",
    "serialize_log_entry",
    "serialize_test_run",

"""Use-case tests against in-memory fakes."""

from __future__ import annotations

from pathlib import Path

import pytest

from mcp_phone_controll.domain.entities import BuildMode, Bounds, Platform, UiElement
from mcp_phone_controll.domain.failures import (
    DeviceNotFoundFailure,
    NoDeviceSelectedFailure,
    UiElementNotFoundFailure,
)
from mcp_phone_controll.domain.result import Err, Ok
from mcp_phone_controll.domain.usecases.base import NoParams
from mcp_phone_controll.domain.usecases.build_install import (
    InstallApp,
    InstallAppParams,
)
from mcp_phone_controll.domain.usecases.devices import (
    GetSelectedDevice,
    ListDevices,
    SelectDevice,
    SelectDeviceParams,
)
from mcp_phone_controll.domain.usecases.lifecycle import (
    LaunchApp,
    LaunchAppParams,
)
from mcp_phone_controll.domain.usecases.ui_query import (
    AssertVisible,
    AssertVisibleParams,
)
from tests.fakes.fake_repositories import (
    FakeArtifactRepository,
    FakeBuildRepository,
    FakeDeviceRepository,
    FakeLifecycleRepository,
    FakeSessionStateRepository,
    FakeUiRepository,
)


@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_list_devices_returns_devices():
    uc = ListDevices(FakeDeviceRepository())
    result = await uc(NoParams())
    assert isinstance(result, Ok)
    assert result.value[0].serial == "EMU01"


@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_select_device_persists_selection():
    devices = FakeDeviceRepository()
    state = FakeSessionStateRepository()
    uc = SelectDevice(devices, state)
    result = await uc(SelectDeviceParams(serial="EMU01"))
    assert isinstance(result, Ok)
    assert state.serial == "EMU01"


@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_select_device_unknown_returns_err():
    uc = SelectDevice(FakeDeviceRepository(), FakeSessionStateRepository())
    result = await uc(SelectDeviceParams(serial="NOPE"))
    assert isinstance(result, Err)
    assert isinstance(result.failure, DeviceNotFoundFailure)


@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_get_selected_device_when_unselected_returns_none():
    uc = GetSelectedDevice(FakeDeviceRepository(), FakeSessionStateRepository())
    result = await uc(NoParams())
    assert isinstance(result, Ok)
    assert result.value is None


@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_install_uses_selected_serial(tmp_path: Path):
    apk = tmp_path / "fake.apk"
    apk.write_bytes(b"X")
    builds = FakeBuildRepository(bundle_path=apk)
    lifecycle = FakeLifecycleRepository()
    devices = FakeDeviceRepository()
    state = FakeSessionStateRepository(serial="EMU01")
    uc = InstallApp(builds, lifecycle, devices, state)

    result = await uc(InstallAppParams(bundle_path=apk))
    assert isinstance(result, Ok)
    assert any(c[1] == "install" and c[2] == "EMU01" for c in lifecycle.calls)


@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_install_without_selected_device_errors(tmp_path: Path):
    builds = FakeBuildRepository()
    lifecycle = FakeLifecycleRepository()
    devices = FakeDeviceRepository()
    state = FakeSessionStateRepository()
    uc = InstallApp(builds, lifecycle, devices, state)

    result = await uc(InstallAppParams(bundle_path=tmp_path / "x.apk"))
    assert isinstance(result, Err)
    assert isinstance(result.failure, NoDeviceSelectedFailure)


@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_install_builds_when_only_project_path_given():
    builds = FakeBuildRepository(bundle_path=Path("/tmp/built.apk"))
    lifecycle = FakeLifecycleRepository()
    devices = FakeDeviceRepository()
    state = FakeSessionStateRepository(serial="EMU01")
    uc = InstallApp(builds, lifecycle, devices, state)

    result = await uc(
        InstallAppParams(project_path=Path("/work/myapp"), mode=BuildMode.DEBUG)
    )
    assert isinstance(result, Ok)
    assert result.value.path == Path("/tmp/built.apk")
    assert result.value.platform is Platform.ANDROID


@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_launch_passes_activity():
    lifecycle = FakeLifecycleRepository()
    state = FakeSessionStateRepository(serial="EMU01")
    uc = LaunchApp(lifecycle, state)

    result = await uc(LaunchAppParams(package_id="com.x", activity=".Main"))
    assert isinstance(result, Ok)
    assert ("launch", "EMU01", "com.x", ".Main") in lifecycle.calls


@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_assert_visible_succeeds_when_found():
    elem = UiElement(
        text="Sign in",
        resource_id=None,
        class_name="android.widget.Button",
        content_description=None,
        bounds=Bounds(0, 0, 100, 50),
        enabled=True,
        clickable=True,
    )
    ui = FakeUiRepository(found=elem)
    state = FakeSessionStateRepository(serial="EMU01")
    uc = AssertVisible(ui, state)
    result = await uc(AssertVisibleParams(text="Sign in"))
    assert isinstance(result, Ok)
    assert result.value is elem


@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_assert_visible_errors_when_missing():
    ui = FakeUiRepository(found=None)
    state = FakeSessionStateRepository(serial="EMU01")
    uc = AssertVisible(ui, state)
    result = await uc(AssertVisibleParams(text="Nope"))
    assert isinstance(result, Err)
    assert isinstance(result.failure, UiElementNotFoundFailure)


@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_artifact_session_returned_by_fake(tmp_path: Path):
    artifacts = FakeArtifactRepository(root=tmp_path)
    res = await artifacts.new_session("smoke")
    assert isinstance(res, Ok)
    assert res.value.label == "smoke"
    assert res.value.root.exists()

[project]
name = "mcp-phone-controll"
version = "0.1.0"
description = "MCP server for building, deploying and testing Flutter apps on real Android devices"
readme = "README.md"
requires-python = ">=3.11"
authors = [{ name = "Michal Giza", email = "msquaregiza@gmail.com" }]
dependencies = [
    "mcp>=1.2.0",
    "uiautomator2>=3.2.0",
    "anyio>=4.4.0",
    "pydantic>=2.7.0",
]

[project.optional-dependencies]
dev = [
    "pytest>=8.0.0",
    "pytest-asyncio>=0.23.0",
    "pytest-cov>=5.0.0",
    "ruff>=0.5.0",
    "mypy>=1.10.0",
]

[project.scripts]
mcp-phone-controll = "mcp_phone_controll.__main__:main"

[build-system]
requires = ["hatchling"]
build-backend = "hatchling.build"

[tool.hatch.build.targets.wheel]
packages = ["src/mcp_phone_controll"]

[tool.pytest.ini_options]
testpaths = ["tests"]
asyncio_mode = "auto"
addopts = "-ra --strict-markers"
python_classes = "Test"
filterwarnings = ["ignore::pytest.PytestCollectionWarning"]

[tool.ruff]
line-length = 100
target-version = "py311"

[tool.ruff.lint]
select = ["E", "F", "I", "B", "UP", "SIM", "RUF"]

[tool.mypy]
python_version = "3.11"
strict = true
packages = ["mcp_phone_controll"]

input.content:
# mcp-phone-controll

A local MCP server that lets Claude Code build, deploy, and exercise Flutter apps on real Android **and** iOS devices through a single uniform tool surface.

## What it does

Same MCP tool names work for both platforms — Claude calls `install_app`, `tap_text`, `read_logs`, `run_integration_tests`, etc. The server figures out which device a serial belongs to and routes to the right backend.

- discover devices over USB (ADB for Android, usbmuxd for iOS)
- build a Flutter project for either platform (`flutter build apk` / `flutter build ipa`)
- install / launch / stop / clear / uninstall apps
- drive the UI: tap, swipe, type, find by text/resource-id, wait, dump tree, assert visible
- observe: screenshot, screen record (Android only), logcat / os_log
- run `flutter test` and `flutter test integration_test/` and report structured results
- group artefacts (screenshots, logs, recordings) per session

## Architecture

Clean Architecture in three layers with a presentation shell:

```
domain/         pure business logic — entities, Result, Failures, repository protocols, use cases
infrastructure/ outbound adapters — adb, flutter CLI, uiautomator2, pymobiledevice3, WebDriverAgent
data/           parsers + repository implementations
data/repositories/composite/   platform-routing composites — same Protocols, route by serial
presentation/   MCP server — tool registry maps tool names to use cases
container.py    composition root — wires every concrete repo behind composites
```

Dependency direction is inward: presentation → domain ← data → infrastructure. Domain knows nothing about ADB, MCP, the filesystem, or any platform.

Errors are returned, not thrown. Every use case returns `Result[T]` (`Ok | Err`) with a typed `Failure`. The MCP layer translates that into a uniform `{ok, data, error}` envelope.

### How the platform routing works

`CachingPlatformResolver` maintains a `serial → Platform` cache. `CompositeDeviceRepository.list_devices()` queries both Android and iOS backends and populates the cache. Every other composite (`CompositeLifecycleRepository`, `CompositeUiRepository`, …) delegates per call by looking up the serial in the resolver.

Use cases never see a `Platform` argument (except `build_app`, which has no serial to resolve from) — they just call the protocol they were given.

## Setup

External prerequisites:

**Android:**
- `adb` on `PATH` (Android Platform Tools)
- USB debugging enabled, host fingerprint authorised
- One-time per device: `python -m uiautomator2 init`

**iOS:**
- macOS host, Xcode + command-line tools installed
- `pymobiledevice3` reachable on `PATH` (installed via the package's deps)
- iOS device trusted to host
- For app launch / on-device tests: Developer Disk Image mounted (`pymobiledevice3 mounter auto-mount`)
- For UI automation: WebDriverAgent built and installed on the device (one-time, via Xcode)
- Code signing configured in the Flutter project (standard iOS dev requirement)

**Both:**
- `flutter` on `PATH`

Install:

```bash
uv venv --python 3.11
source .venv/bin/activate
uv pip install -e ".[dev]"
```

Run tests (no phone needed — everything below the repository boundary is faked):

```bash
pytest
```

Register with Claude Code:

```bash
claude mcp add phone-controll -- python -m mcp_phone_controll
```

## Tools (28 total)

All tools are uniform across Android and iOS. Where a tool isn't supported on a platform (e.g. screen recording on iOS, `clear_data` / `grant_permission` on iOS), the call returns a typed `Failure` with a hint.

`build_app` and `install_app` accept an optional `platform` parameter. `install_app` defaults to the selected device's platform.

## Layout

```
src/mcp_phone_controll/
  domain/
    result.py / failures.py / entities.py / repositories.py
    usecases/                  one class per action, extending BaseUseCase
  infrastructure/
    process_runner.py          single async-subprocess chokepoint
    adb_client.py              Android wrapper
    flutter_cli.py             flutter CLI (build apk + build ipa + test)
    uiautomator2_factory.py    Android UI client cache
    pymobiledevice3_cli.py     iOS device CLI wrapper
    wda_factory.py             iOS UI client (facebook-wda over usbmux)
  data/
    parsers/                   adb-devices / logcat / pymobiledevice3 usbmux / flutter test JSON
    repositories/
      adb_*.py                 Android implementations
      ios_*.py / wda_*.py      iOS implementations
      flutter_*.py             cross-platform Flutter (build, test)
      composite/               platform-routing composites
  presentation/
    serialization.py / tool_registry.py / mcp_server.py
  container.py                 composition root
  __main__.py                  entrypoint
```

## Testing strategy

- Use cases are tested against fake repositories — no subprocess, no phone.
- Parsers (adb-devices, logcat, flutter JSON reporter, pymobiledevice3 usbmux) are pure functions with fixture tests.
- Composite routing is tested with fakes: confirms platform resolution, per-serial dispatch, and that an iOS toolchain failure does not break the Android path.
- The tool registry is tested end-to-end with fake use cases to verify schema/dispatch.
- Real ADB / uiautomator2 / pymobiledevice3 / WDA repositories are exercised manually with a phone.

`pytest` runs the full 44-test suite in under a second.

## What's deferred

- iOS screen recording (requires `xrecord` or QuickTime — not exposed by pymobiledevice3).
- True streaming of `pymobiledevice3 syslog live` — currently approximated via a windowed `syslog collect`.
- Wi-Fi ADB, emulator parallel runs, network conditioning, signing/release flows.

- True streaming of `pymobiledevice3 syslog live` — currently approximated via a windowed `syslog collect`.
- Wi-Fi ADB, emulator parallel runs, network conditioning, signing/release flows.

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# mcp-phone-controll

A local MCP server that lets Claude Code build, deploy, and exercise Flutter apps on a real Android phone end-to-end.

## What it does

Exposes tools to Claude for the full on-device loop:

- discover devices over USB ADB
- build a Flutter project (`flutter build apk`)
- install / launch / stop / clear / uninstall apps
- drive the UI: tap, swipe, type, find by text/resource-id, wait, dump tree, assert visible
- observe: screenshot, screen record, logcat read & tail
- run `flutter test` and `flutter test integration_test/` and report structured results
- group artefacts (screenshots, logs, recordings) per session

## Architecture

Clean Architecture in three layers with a presentation shell:

```
domain/         pure business logic — entities, Result, Failures, repository protocols, use cases
infrastructure/ outbound adapters — adb subprocess, flutter CLI, uiautomator2 client
data/           repository implementations + parsers; depend on infrastructure, implement domain protocols
presentation/   MCP server — tool registry maps tool names to use cases, serialises results
container.py    composition root — wires concrete repos into use cases
```

Dependency direction is inward: presentation → domain ← data → infrastructure. Domain knows nothing about ADB, MCP, or the filesystem.

Errors are returned, not thrown. Every use case returns `Result[T]` (`Ok | Err`) with a typed `Failure`. The MCP layer translates that into a uniform `{ok, data, error}` envelope.

## Setup

External prerequisites:

- `adb` on `PATH` (Android Platform Tools)
- `flutter` on `PATH`
- Phone with USB debugging enabled and host fingerprint authorised
- One-time per device: `python -m uiautomator2 init` (installs the on-device agent)

Install:

```bash
uv venv
source .venv/bin/activate
uv pip install -e ".[dev]"
```

Run tests (no phone needed — everything below the repository boundary is faked):

```bash
pytest
```

Register with Claude Code:

```bash
claude mcp add phone-controll -- python -m mcp_phone_controll
```

## Layout

```
src/mcp_phone_controll/
  domain/
    result.py            Ok / Err / Result
    failures.py          Failure hierarchy
    entities.py          Device, ApkArtifact, UiElement, LogEntry, TestResult, ...
    repositories.py      Protocols (Device, Lifecycle, Build, Ui, Observation, Test, Artifact, SessionStore)
    usecases/            One class per action, all extending BaseUseCase
  infrastructure/
    process_runner.py    async subprocess primitive (single chokepoint)
    adb_client.py        thin async adb wrapper
    flutter_cli.py       async flutter CLI wrapper
    uiautomator2_factory.py  lazy uiautomator2.Device factory
  data/
    parsers/             pure functions: adb devices output, flutter test JSON reporter
    repositories/        concrete implementations of domain protocols
  presentation/
    serialization.py     entity → JSON
    tool_registry.py     declarative tool descriptor → use case mapping
    mcp_server.py        wires registry to the MCP SDK
  container.py           composition root
  __main__.py            entrypoint
```

## Testing strategy

- Use cases are tested against fake repositories — no subprocess, no phone.
- Parsers are pure functions tested with captured fixture output.
- The tool registry is tested end-to-end with fake use cases to verify schema/dispatch.
- The real ADB / uiautomator2 / Flutter CLI repositories are exercised manually with a phone.

content.text:
iOS support is in. **44 tests pass, 28 tools wired, both platforms behind one set of MCP tool names.** Now the testing skill.

A Claude Code skill is a Markdown file at `~/.claude/skills/<name>/SKILL.md` with frontmatter declaring when it triggers. The user wants a skill that, when invoked while testing apps via this MCP, walks Claude through a rigorous testing methodology — including cross-checking that test cases are valid for the use case (vs needing a rewrite).

Let me design the skill carefully. Best-practice testing methodology should cover:

1. **Pre-flight** — environment, device, app state.
2. **Requirement traceability** — every test case must map to a requirement / acceptance criterion.
3. **Test case validity heuristics** — what makes a test case "valid for this use case" vs. needing rewrite (clear AC, deterministic, isolated, observable, repeatable, atomic, etc.).
4. **Test pyramid for on-device** — unit → widget → integration → smoke → e2e on real hardware.
5. **MCP-specific patterns** — how to use the tools we built (golden-path loop: install → launch → wait_for → assert_visible → screenshot → read_logs).
6. **Failure triage** — what each Failure code means and where to look next.
7. **Cross-platform parity** — how to validate behaviour matches across Android & iOS.

name: flutter-ui-linter
description: Pre-handover UI linter for Flutter apps. Catches layout crashes, overflow traps, design-system violations, lifecycle leaks, and consent-ordering issues BEFORE they reach a reviewer. Runs static analysis on `lib/**`, reports severity-classified findings, and suggests fixes with good/bad examples.

# Flutter UI Linter

## Purpose

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                FLUTTER UI LINTER — WHY THIS EXISTS              │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                 │
│   Most Flutter UI bugs are a handful of patterns repeating:     │
│   • Row / Column asking children for infinite height            │
│   • CrossAxisAlignment.stretch inside a scroll view             │
│   • Continuous animations that break pumpAndSettle              │
│   • Raw Color(0xFF…) colours that can't be re-themed            │
│   • Temp files deleted before the consumer reads them           │
│   • MobileAds.initialize() firing before Apple ATT              │
│                                                                 │
│   This skill catches ALL of them, cites the exact line, shows   │
│   a good/bad example, and refuses to mark work "done" until     │
│   they're resolved.                                             │
│                                                                 │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

## When to use

input.content:
---
name: mcp-phone-controll-testing
description: On-device Flutter testing methodology using the mcp-phone-controll MCP server. Use when planning, executing, or reviewing tests run against a real Android or iOS device through the MCP tools (list_devices, install_app, tap_text, run_integration_tests, screenshot, read_logs, etc.). Provides a Test Validity Rubric to decide which test cases are valid for the requirement and which need to be rewritten before they're worth running, plus a failure-triage cheat sheet keyed to the MCP's Failure codes. Also use when the user asks to verify acceptance criteria on hardware, build a regression suite, or audit an existing test plan against a real device run.
---

# On-Device Testing — mcp-phone-controll

## Purpose

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│      WHY THIS SKILL EXISTS                                      │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                 │
│  The MCP gives Claude superpowers on a real phone. Without a    │
│  methodology those superpowers produce noisy, flaky, "ran but   │
│  proves nothing" tests. This skill keeps every on-device run    │
│  tied to:                                                       │
│                                                                 │
│   1. A specific requirement / acceptance criterion              │
│   2. A test case that meets the Validity Rubric                 │
│   3. An MCP tool sequence that produces evidence Claude can     │
│      cite (screenshot path, log line, structured TestRun)       │
│   4. A failure triage path keyed to typed Failure codes         │
│   5. A cross-platform parity check when the feature ships on    │
│      both Android and iOS                                       │
│                                                                 │
│  If a test case can't pass the Rubric, REWRITE IT before        │
│  running. A red test that proves nothing is worse than no test. │
│                                                                 │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

## When to use

Trigger this skill when the user is:

- planning a test run that will hit a real device through `mcp-phone-controll`
- writing or reviewing `integration_test/` Flutter tests destined for hardware
- auditing an existing test plan ("are these still valid?")
- triaging a failed `run_integration_tests` result
- adding cross-platform coverage (Android-tested feature now needs iOS sign-off)
- building a regression checklist for release sign-off

Skip this skill for:

- Pure unit tests (use the standard `flutter-testing` skill — they don't need a device).
- Widget tests that run in `flutter test` without a device.
- One-off "click around and see" exploration where evidence isn't required.

## The Test Validity Rubric

Every on-device test case must pass **all** of these before it's worth running. If any item is "no", REWRITE the case (or split it).

| # | Criterion             | Concrete check                                                              |
|---|-----------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| 1 | **Mapped to AC**      | The case names the exact acceptance criterion or requirement ID it covers. |
| 2 | **Single behaviour**  | One `Given/When/Then`. Multiple `When`s = split.                            |
| 3 | **Deterministic**     | No reliance on real time, network, randomness, or device-specific defaults. |
| 4 | **Observable via MCP**| The "Then" can be verified by `assert_visible`, `read_logs` regex, or a `TestRun` field — not just "looks right". |
| 5 | **Isolated**          | Runs after `clear_app_data` (or fresh install) — no carry-over from prior tests. |
| 6 | **Idempotent setup**  | Re-running the case from scratch yields identical evidence.                 |
| 7 | **Bounded waits**     | Every wait has a numeric `timeout_s`. No "sleep until it works" fallback.   |
| 8 | **Cross-platform safe** | Uses semantic selectors (`text=`, `resource_id=`) — not raw coordinates that drift across screen sizes. |
| 9 | **Failure-aware**     | The case states what failure mode it would catch (so a regression in that mode actually breaks it). |
| 10| **Cheap diagnosis**   | On failure, captures `screenshot` + `read_logs(min_level="W")` so triage doesn't need a re-run. |

**Rewrite signals** — if you see any of these, the test case is invalid, not the code:

- "Test passes locally, fails in CI" with no further detail → criterion 3 fails.
- Uses `Future.delayed(Duration(seconds: 5))` to wait for UI → criterion 7.
- Asserts `find.byType(Container)` count → criterion 4 (not behaviour, just structure).
- Depends on a previous test having logged in → criterion 5.
- Asserts pixel positions → criterion 8.
- "It's just a smoke test" with no AC mapped → criterion 1.

## Requirement → Test Traceability

Before adding tests, build (or have the user supply) a 3-column matrix:

| AC ID | Acceptance criterion (one sentence) | Test case ID(s) covering it |
|-------|------------------------------------|------------------------------|
| AC-1  | A signed-in user sees their email on the home screen. | TC-HOME-01 |
| AC-2  | Tapping "Sign out" returns to the login screen within 2s. | TC-AUTH-07 |

Rules:

- Every AC needs **≥ 1** test case. If it doesn't, flag the gap, don't invent the test silently.
- Every test case needs **exactly 1** AC. Multi-AC cases are too coarse to localise failures.
- ACs without a clear "Then" observable through MCP are a **specification bug**, not a test problem — flag it.

## Test pyramid for this MCP

```
                        ┌──────────────┐
                        │  smoke (5)   │  ← run on every commit on hardware
                        │  golden flow │
                        └──────────────┘
                     ┌──────────────────┐
                     │ integration (~30)│  ← `run_integration_tests`
                     │  per-feature     │     on hardware before release
                     └──────────────────┘
                ┌──────────────────────────┐
                │     widget (~hundreds)   │  ← `flutter test` — no MCP
                └──────────────────────────┘
            ┌──────────────────────────────────┐
            │       unit (~thousands)          │  ← `flutter test` — no MCP
            └──────────────────────────────────┘
```

Decide where a case belongs:

- **Pure logic / pure mapping** → unit. Don't waste a phone for it.
- **Widget rendering, no I/O** → widget test in-process.
- **Real BLoC + real repos + real platform channel** → integration on hardware.
- **Whole user journey (sign in → action → sign out)** → smoke flow on hardware.

If a unit-level concern is being verified at integration level, that's a **misplaced test** — recommend moving it down. Conversely, if a hardware-only concern (real ATT prompt, real biometric) is being mocked at widget level, recommend moving it up.

## MCP loop patterns

Every on-device test case should follow one of these shapes. Pick the smallest that proves the AC.

### A) Smoke install loop (criteria 1–10)

```
new_session(label="smoke-<sha>")
list_devices                       → confirm at least one device
select_device(serial=...)
install_app(project_path=..., mode="debug")
launch_app(package_id=...)
wait_for_element(text="<known boot text>", timeout_s=15)
take_screenshot(label="post-launch")
read_logs(min_level="W", since_s=10)   → assert no FATAL/E in app's tag
```

If `wait_for_element` times out → the build is broken; do **not** write more cases until this loop is green.

### B) Single-AC integration case

```
clear_app_data(package_id=...)        ← criterion 5
launch_app(package_id=...)
wait_for_element(<entry point>, timeout_s=10)
... drive to the screen under test using tap_text/type_text ...
assert_visible(<expected element>, timeout_s=5)   ← the "Then"
take_screenshot(label="ac-<id>-pass")
```

### C) Negative path

Same as B, but the "Then" is `assert_visible(<error/empty state>)`. Capture both the screenshot AND a `read_logs(tag="<feature>", min_level="W")` so the failure mode is recorded as evidence.

### D) Long-running async ("after X happens, Y appears")

```
tap_text("Submit")
tail_logs(until_pattern="job_completed=true", tag="myapp", timeout_s=30)
assert_visible(<success toast>, timeout_s=5)
```

Never poll `find_element` in a loop yourself — use `wait_for_element` or `tail_logs`.

### E) Suite run (CI-style)

```
new_session(label="release-<sha>")
list_devices
select_device(serial=...)
install_app(project_path=..., mode="profile")
run_integration_tests(project_path=..., test_path="integration_test/")
```

Inspect the returned `TestRun`. Attach failures to AC IDs in your report. If `errored > 0`, treat as infrastructure failure (criterion 3 violation), not a code regression — re-run before filing.

## Failure-triage cheat sheet

Every MCP envelope carries `error.code`. Use it to decide the next move — don't guess.

| Code                        | Most likely cause                                  | Next MCP tool to call                            |
|-----------------------------|----------------------------------------------------|--------------------------------------------------|
| `NoDeviceSelectedFailure`   | Skipped `select_device`.                           | `list_devices` → `select_device`.                |
| `DeviceNotFoundFailure`     | Cable popped, device locked, or never listed.      | `list_devices`. If absent, ask user to check USB. |
| `DeviceOfflineFailure`      | Device authorised this host before but is now off.  | Ask user to unlock + re-tap "Trust".             |
| `AdbFailure`                | Raw adb non-zero exit. Read `details.stderr`.      | If `unauthorized` → re-trust. If `device offline` → reconnect. |
| `BuildFailure`              | `flutter build` failed.                            | Stop. Read `details.stderr`. Don't run more tests until build is green. |
| `InstallFailure`            | APK signature mismatch / older version installed.  | `uninstall_app` then retry `install_app`.        |
| `LaunchFailure` (iOS)       | Developer Disk Image not mounted.                  | Ask user to run `pymobiledevice3 mounter auto-mount`. |
| `UiElementNotFoundFailure`  | Selector wrong, screen changed, or app crashed.    | `take_screenshot` + `dump_ui` + `read_logs`.     |
| `TimeoutFailure`            | Wait was tight, or the trigger never fired.        | Increase `timeout_s` once. If still failing, treat as real bug. |
| `UiFailure`                 | UIA2 / WDA disconnected.                           | Re-`list_devices` (forces reconnect via factory). |
| `TestExecutionFailure`      | `flutter test` produced no JSON events.            | Read `details.stderr`. Often a missing dep or invalid `pubspec`. |
| `FilesystemFailure`         | Artifact dir not writable.                         | `new_session` again or check `~/.mcp_phone_controll/`. |
| `InvalidArgumentFailure`    | Bad/missing tool argument.                         | Re-read schema; do not retry blindly.            |
| `UnexpectedFailure`         | Bug in the MCP server itself.                      | Capture `details`, surface to the user, do not retry. |

## Cross-platform parity protocol

When a feature must work on both Android and iOS, run the **same** test case against each device and compare. Use the same selectors (`text=`, `resource_id=`) — they map to UIA2 (`text` / `resourceId`) on Android and WDA (`labelContains` / `name`) on iOS via the composite repos.

Parity checklist:

1. The test passes on both serials in the same `new_session`.
2. The screenshots show the same screen at the same step (manually compare; deferred: visual diff).
3. The log evidence (`read_logs`) shows the same business-event tags on both platforms.
4. Differences in `os_version` are noted in the report.
5. Tools that aren't supported on iOS (`clear_app_data`, `grant_permission`, `start_recording`) are replaced with a documented workaround in the test plan — **never silently skipped**.

If a test passes on one platform but is "not applicable" on the other, that's a spec answer (e.g. "iOS uses Face ID instead of fingerprint") — record the AC mapping for both platforms separately rather than claiming parity.

## Reporting

Every test session run through this skill ends with a report block:

```
SESSION:    <session_id>            ← from `new_session`
DEVICE:     <serial> (<platform> <os_version>)
ARTIFACTS:  <path>                  ← from `get_artifacts_dir`

PASSED:    <n>
FAILED:    <n>
ERRORED:   <n>
SKIPPED:   <n>  (reasons must be listed)

AC COVERAGE
  AC-1  ✓  TC-HOME-01
  AC-2  ✗  TC-AUTH-07     →  see screenshots/auth-fail.png + logs/auth-fail.log
  AC-3  ⚠  no test         →  GAP

REWRITES NEEDED
  TC-XYZ-04 — fails Rubric criterion 3 (uses Future.delayed)
  TC-XYZ-09 — fails Rubric criterion 1 (no AC mapped)

TRIAGE
  TC-AUTH-07 → TimeoutFailure on wait_for("Welcome", 10s)
              → screenshot shows network error toast
              → read_logs: "AuthRepo: 401 from /me"
              → Likely real bug, not test-side.
```

The report is the deliverable. Without it, "ran the tests on a phone" is not evidence anyone can act on.

## Quick decision tree

```
Is this an AC or a hunch?
  hunch  → don't write a hardware test; explore manually first
  AC     ↓

Can a unit/widget test prove it?
  yes    → push it down the pyramid; don't use the MCP
  no     ↓

Does it pass all 10 Rubric criteria?
  no     → REWRITE before running
  yes    ↓

Pick MCP loop A/B/C/D/E
Run on Android serial
Cross-platform feature?
  yes    → run identical case on iOS serial; compare evidence
  no     ↓

Produce the SESSION report. Map every result to its AC.
```

## What this skill will NOT do

- Decide for you whether a feature ships. The skill produces evidence; the human owns the call.
- Pretend a flaky test is "intermittent". Flake = Rubric criterion 3 violation = rewrite.
- Run destructive operations (factory reset, `uninstall` on production-data apps) without explicit user confirmation.
- Silently mock anything for a "hardware" run. Mocks belong below this layer; if a hardware test needs a mock, the test is misplaced.
